name: Release hardening cross-functional collaboration
purpose: Coordinate research, execution, sustainment, protection, red team, and recorder outputs so release hardening is executed without authority or source drift.

responsibilities: Integrate department outputs into commander-readable decision packets.
Resolve sequencing conflicts inside mission scope.
authority_boundary: Must escalate Red approval, external release, high risk acceptance, and scope change.
ccir: Any department output contradicts commander intent.
Any support dependency blocks main effort.

responsibilities: Verify official sources and source conflicts.
authority_boundary: May not authorize final release or scope change.
ccir: Primary source conflict changes the release decision.
No official source supports a critical claim.

responsibilities: Convert commander intent into task sequence.
Run backbrief and rehearsal before risky execution.
authority_boundary: Must escalate scope and authority changes.
ccir: Execution path requires unapproved Red action.

responsibilities: Maintain tool, context, fallback, and documentation readiness.
Route degraded assets to commander-facing queue.
authority_boundary: May mark tools degraded or unavailable.
May not approve release or accept high residual risk.
ccir: Critical runner or source-map linter unavailable.

responsibilities: Apply EEFI, context releasability, release review, and rollback controls.
authority_boundary: May not approve external release on behalf of Commander.
ccir: Reviewed target differs from actual release target.
Restricted or EEFI context appears in final output.

responsibilities: Identify contradiction, abuse case, hallucination, and unbounded authority risk.
Recommend no-go when control gaps remain.
authority_boundary: May not execute or approve release.
ccir: Assumption is being treated as sourced fact.

responsibilities: Maintain event log, source of truth, handoff, and AAR inputs.
Preserve decisions and unresolved risks for the next worker.
authority_boundary: May require missing handoff state before phase close.
ccir: Source of truth diverges from executed state.
Handoff packet is missing before role transition.

required_outputs: Source-map delta linked to research claim.
quality_gate: Official source host is covered by source-map linter.
escalation_trigger: Official source is missing or conflicts with current doctrine.

required_outputs: Sourced constraints and assumptions for the execution plan.
quality_gate: All mission-changing claims are tagged as source-backed or assumption.
escalation_trigger: Low-confidence information would change task order.

quality_gate: Critical runners and source-map linter are ready or degraded with fallback.
escalation_trigger: Required tool is unavailable and no fallback exists.

quality_gate: No unsupported claim is presented as fact.
escalation_trigger: Final output contains claim without source or assumption tag.

required_outputs: Critical failure modes and residual risk note.
quality_gate: Critical finding is resolved, accepted by Commander, or release is blocked.
escalation_trigger: Critical or high residual risk remains.

required_outputs: Executed steps, skipped steps, failed checks, and next action.
quality_gate: Next worker can resume without reading conversation history.
escalation_trigger: Executed state and documented state diverge.

information_to_exchange: Source URLs, claim IDs, source-map row, unresolved questions.
conflict_route: S2 source review, then CoS decision packet if doctrine conflict remains.

information_to_exchange: Task constraints, assumptions, confidence, order-changing findings.
liaison_rules.update_cadence: before task order and after rehearsal friction
conflict_route: CoS resolves sequence; Commander decides scope changes.

information_to_exchange: Required runner, tool availability, fallback, context budget.
liaison_rules.update_cadence: before execution and after tool failure
conflict_route: CoS priority decision; Commander if degraded state affects end state.

information_to_exchange: Release candidate, claim-source trace, EEFI handling, target metadata.
conflict_route: Protection blocks release; Commander decides release exception.

information_to_exchange: Critical findings, mitigations, residual risk, release constraints.
Executed actions, skipped checks, unresolved blockers, next action.

method: CoS resolves sequencing and dependency conflicts inside mission scope.
Commander decides Red approval, release approval, high risk acceptance, and scope FRAGO.
Conflicts touching source validity, release target, or authority boundary require decision packet.
commander_escalation: High or critical residual risk remains.
Mission scope or authority boundary changes.

need_to_know: Departments receive the minimum context needed for their output contract.
context_sharing: Raw source goes to research and recorder.
Release reviewer receives redacted final candidate and releasability metadata.
eefi_controls: Credential-like values are summarized, not copied.
Restricted context is denied from final output.
Release review target must match actual release target.

success: Every relationship output is delivered and quality-gated.
All commander decision points are resolved or explicitly deferred.
Handoff packet names source of truth and unresolved risk.
abort: No liaison exists for a blocking dependency.
Shared source of truth diverges from execution state.
handoff: Recorder captures event log, decision packet status, changed files, source-map deltas, and next action.
